headline: Palantir worst month in years despite AI boom
detail: The headline confirms PLTR's weakness isn't just noise — sentiment has genuinely turned on the name even as AI stays hot broadly. You're already down -17.56% and on margin, a continued slide to your $85 stop represents a meaningful realized loss. Watch closely for any stabilization or cut the position before the stop forces your hand.

headline: Iran tightens Strait of Hormuz, oil jumps
detail: Rising oil from a geopolitical flashpoint adds inflation pressure and increases the odds the Fed stays tighter longer. That directly compresses tech and semiconductor multiples — bad news for PLTR, AVGO, SMH, and CRWD all at once. On margin, a broad sector reprice from macro shock is the scenario you least want.

headline: Apple and Microsoft hit by soaring component costs
detail: Both companies citing higher component costs signals that input-price pressure is real and spreading across big tech hardware. AVGO, as a major chip supplier, sits right in the crosshairs — customers facing cost pressure tend to trim orders. This adds a fundamental headwind to your largest position already down -13.18%.

headline: Fed's Goolsbee says inflation still too high
detail: Goolsbee's hawkish tone pushes back against any near-term rate-cut hopes, which is a direct headwind for high-multiple tech and growth names that dominate your book. Combined with oil spiking, the macro backdrop today is unfriendly to exactly the concentrated, margin-leveraged tech portfolio you're running.
